sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Levante UD have shown mixed form in their last 5 matches, managing a win, a draw, and three losses, with a notable 3-2 victory against Elche. Valencia, on the other hand, are struggling, having lost three of their last five matches, including a 2-0 defeat to Real Madrid, though they secured a 3-2 win against Espanyol recently. Both teams are in desperate need of points to climb out of the relegation zone.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In their last two encounters, Valencia have the upper hand, with a 1-0 win in November 2025 and a 1-1 draw back in 2022. Levante have struggled to secure a victory against Valencia in recent head-to-head matchups, failing to win in their last five meetings, which could impact their confidence going into this match.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Levante UD, sitting 19th with 18 points, have scored 26 goals but conceded 38, showing defensive frailties. Valencia, in 17th with 23 points, have also struggled defensively, conceding 37 goals while scoring only 23. Both teams rank poorly in possession and passing accuracy, suggesting a scrappy, counter-attacking game might ensue.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Absences
sections.text: Levante's Etta Eyong (6 goals) and Iván Romero will be crucial for their attack, while missing players like Jeremy Toljan could weaken their defense. Valencia's Hugo Duro (7 goals) is a key threat, but his potential absence alongside Arnaut Danjuma might hinder their firepower. Both sides will need their stand-ins to step up.

sections.subtitle: Match Outlook
sections.text: With Sofascore predicting a 41% chance of a Levante win, 27% draw, and 32% Valencia win, this match appears closely contested. Levante might have a slight edge at home, but Valencia's historical dominance in head-to-heads could play a psychological role. Expect a tight, low-scoring affair given both teams' recent defensive struggles and low corner averages.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Levante UD and Valencia ended in a goalless draw at the Ciudad de Valencia. Neither side managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Levante UD showed more intent in creating significant opportunities, while Valencia held a slight edge in ball possession. Both teams entered the final third frequently, but lacked the clinical finishing needed to convert chances into goals. Defensive solidity has been a key factor so far, with both sides holding firm under pressure.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attacking momentum
halftimeSections.text: The game started with Levante UD pushing forward with early attacking intent, showing dominance in the opening minutes. However, momentum quickly shifted to Valencia, who enjoyed a sustained period of control with several threatening spells in the attacking third. Mid-way through the half, Levante UD regained the initiative, mounting pressure with a series of dangerous advances. Towards the latter stages, momentum fluctuated between the two sides, with Valencia showing brief surges, only for Levante to close the half on a stronger note with renewed attacking energy.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key team statistics
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD created more big chances in the first half, but their inability to convert left them frustrated. They also demonstrated strength in duels, particularly on the ground, which allowed them to win possession in crucial areas. Valencia, on the other hand, controlled the ball for longer periods but failed to translate that into meaningful threats in front of goal. Their entries into the penalty area were comparable to Levante's, yet they lacked the sharpness to test the opposition goalkeeper. Both teams were evenly matched in aerial battles, showing no clear dominance in the air.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Standout players
halftimeSections.text: For Levante UD, Iván Romero has been a notable presence up front, showing intent with his positioning and involvement in key moments. Pablo Martínez also stood out with his creativity, playing a pivotal role in setting up opportunities. On the Valencia side, Stole Dimitrievski was impressive in goal, maintaining composure under pressure. Pepelu showed flashes of quality in midfield, distributing the ball effectively and looking to unlock the Levante defense with precise passes. Hugo Duro remains a potential threat for Valencia, waiting to capitalize on any defensive lapse.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Tactical observations
halftimeSections.text: Levante UD seems to be focusing on exploiting spaces in the final third, with a clear intent to create high-quality chances through quick transitions. However, their finishing needs improvement to make the most of these opportunities. Valencia’s approach has been more possession-based, aiming to wear down the opposition, but they’ve struggled to penetrate the Levante backline with purpose. A yellow card picked up by a Valencia player could also influence their approach in the second half, as they may need to tread carefully to avoid further disciplinary issues. Both managers might consider tactical tweaks to inject more attacking flair after the break.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Second half expectations
halftimeSections.text: The second half promises to be an intriguing battle as both teams search for the opening goal. Levante UD will likely continue to press for chances, given their more dangerous moments in the first half, and could capitalize if they sharpen their finishing. Valencia might look to leverage their possession advantage to create clearer opportunities, potentially introducing fresh legs to add dynamism. Defensive organization will be crucial, as a single lapse could decide this tight contest. The home crowd at Ciudad de Valencia could play a significant role in lifting Levante UD to push for a breakthrough.
